verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 (HSP60) is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin. However, it has well-documented cell-state-induced surface display: it translocates to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane on tumor cells (cancer ecto-chaperone), on cells undergoing heat shock/stress, and on apoptotic/ICD cells. Surface HSP60 on tumor cells is recognized by NK cells and has been targeted by antibody-based therapeutic strategies. This non-baseline outer-leaflet pool qualifies as contextual/cell_state_induced despite the dominant mitochondrial localization.
